paper_title: Allergy Database Analysis and Patient Characterization

methods.description: Retrospective scanning of an anonymized allergy database to identify patients with positive skin prick test (SPT) to birch, filter based on specific IgE (sIgE) results for Bet v 1 and profilin, and divide them into distinct groups based on their serological patterns for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Retrospectively scanned this database for patients who had a positive skin prick test (SPT) to birch between September 2002 and April 2018.
steps.searchable_summary: query allergy database for patients with positive birch SPT within a date range
outputs: list of patients with positive birch SPT

steps.description: A total of 1148 patients had received diagnostics for specific IgE to the allergen components Bet v 1 and profilin (Bet v 2 and/or Phl p 12).
steps.searchable_summary: filter patients based on availability of sIgE diagnostics for Bet v 1 and profilin
inputs: list of patients with positive birch SPT
outputs: list of patients with positive birch SPT and sIgE diagnostics for Bet v 1/profilin

steps.description: Based on the serological pattern, we divided the patient collective into the following groups: Bet v 1 positive and profilin negative (group A, n=800 patients), Bet v 1 positive and profilin positive (group B, n=121 patients), as control group Bet v 1 negative and profilin negative (group C, n=198 patients) and Bet v 1 negative and profilin positive (group D, n=29).
steps.searchable_summary: categorize patients into groups based on Bet v 1 and profilin sIgE results
inputs: list of patients with sIgE results
outputs: patient groups (A, B, C, D) based on sIgE patterns

steps.description: As we wanted to have a look at the relevance of profilin in context of birch pollen allergic patients, individuals with characteristics "Bet v 1 -/profilin +" were not informative in our context, and therefore this group was not analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude group D (Bet v 1 negative/profilin positive) from analysis
outputs: patient groups for analysis (A, B, C)

steps.description: The final study cohort included 1119 patients.
steps.searchable_summary: define the final study cohort size
inputs: patient groups for analysis (A, B, C)

methods.description: Standardized procedure for performing skin prick tests using specific allergen solutions and controls, with defined criteria for reading and classifying positive results.

steps.description: For SPT the solutions by ALK-Abelló, Wedel, Germany had been used.
steps.searchable_summary: use ALK-Abelló SPT solutions

steps.description: We had performed SPT to birch, hazel, alder, ash, timothy grass, rye, mugwort, pellitory, ragweed, Dermatophagoides pteronyssinus, Dermatophagoides farinae, dog, cat, Alternaria and Aspergillus.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SPT for a specified panel of allergens

steps.description: in combination with histamine dihydrochloride solution at 1 mg/mL as positive control
steps.searchable_summary: use histamine solution as positive control for SPT

steps.description: and allergen-free saline solution as negative control
steps.searchable_summary: use saline solution as negative control for SPT

steps.description: read 20 min after application.
steps.searchable_summary: read SPT results after 20 minutes

steps.description: The SPT has been considered positive with a wheal ≥3 mm in diameter
steps.searchable_summary: define positive SPT result based on wheal diameter threshold

steps.description: (divided into the following categories: ≥3 to 4 mm; ≥4 to 5 mm; ≥5 to 6; ≥6 mm)
steps.searchable_summary: classify positive SPT results into categories based on wheal size ranges

steps.description: The procedure and classification were in line with European standards and published guidelines. 13,14
steps.searchable_summary: ensure SPT procedure and classification comply with standards/guidelines

methods.description: Method used to detect and quantify specific IgE (sIgE) antibodies to allergen components Bet v 1 and profilin using a commercially available test kit.

steps.description: The FEIA (fluorescence enzyme immunoassay) method was used to detect IgE reactivity to allergen components Bet v 1 and profilin (Bet v 2 and/or Phl p 12)
steps.searchable_summary: perform FEIA to detect sIgE to Bet v 1 and profilin

steps.description: with a commercially available test kit (UniCAP, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Freiburg, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: use UniCAP test kit for FEIA

steps.description: All procedures were in accordance with the manufacturer'ś instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: follow manufacturer's instructions for FEIA procedure

steps.description: Results were reported as concentrations (kU/L).
steps.searchable_summary: report FEIA results as sIgE concentrations in kU/L

methods.description: Collection of patient medical history, demographic data, and detailed symptom information using standardized questionnaires and interviews.

steps.description: Further, the medical history was registered, partially by a standardized clinical history questionnaire containing demographic data, living environment, complaints of the upper and lower airways, gastrointestinal symptoms and oral allergy symptoms.
steps.searchable_summary: collect medical history and demographic/symptom data using a standardized questionnaire

steps.description: The clinical symptoms were assessed by a standardized questionnaire (an extended version of the standardized German adapted version of the Rhinoconjunctivitis Quality of Life Questionnaire by Juniper).
steps.searchable_summary: assess clinical symptoms using an extended RQLQ questionnaire

steps.description: Oral allergy syndrome was verified by means of case history only by using a standardized interview.
steps.searchable_summary: verify oral allergy syndrome diagnosis via standardized interview

steps.description: We documented patient-reported immediate adverse reactions after ingestion of plant foods.
steps.searchable_summary: document patient-reported immediate adverse food reactions

steps.description: Overall, the following fields of interest were interrogated: diagnosis from the allergic symptom complex, general experience with food, reactions to food, allergic rhinitis and atopic comorbidities ( Table 3).
steps.searchable_summary: structure collected data according to predefined fields of interest
outputs: structured patient data (diagnosis, food experience, food reactions, rhinitis, comorbidities)

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected patient data, including software used and the specific test applied to compare groups.

steps.description: Statistical analysis was performed using R, version 3.5.1 (R Core Team (2018), Vienna, Austria) and Excel (Microsoft, Redmond, WA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using R and Excel software

steps.description: The parametric Pearson's chi-square test was utilized for testing statistically significant differences between the groups.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Pearson's chi-square test to compare groups

steps.description: A p-value of <0.05 was considered significant.
steps.searchable_summary: define statistical significance threshold
